q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Jinja2 Template().render() with user-controlled template source enables SSTI, which can escalate to RCE via Python object traversal — unsafe to execute in tests.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the Jinja2 Template constructor or its render method so that Template("Result: " + fragment).render() does not actually interpret the template source.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the exact template-source string passed to the Template constructor (i.e., the value of "Result: " + fragment) to verify it contains user-controlled input without sanitization.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a benign string such as "Result: mock_rendered" so that downstream HttpResponse creation proceeds normally without executing any template expressions.
notes: The injection is in the template SOURCE, not in render-context variables, so patching jinja2.Template or the import used on line 124 is appropriate.
Ensure the mock captures the full source string to enable assertions that the tainted fragment reaches the sink unsanitized.
